explanation: The property states that globally, whenever the PSV mode is selected by the user (indicating a switch), the PCV mode must be active. This directly translates the requirement that switching from PCV to PSV can only occur while PCV ventilation is currently active.

The property states that globally, if the PSV mode is selected by the user while the PCV ventilation mode is currently active, then in the very next state, the system shall transition to the PSV mode. This accurately captures the requirement of switching from PCV to PSV while PCV is active.

The property states that it is always the case that if a switch to PSV mode is initiated (PSVModeSelected), then PCV ventilation must be active at that moment (PCVMode). This captures the requirement that the 'means to switch' are provided specifically when PCV ventilation is active, meaning the switch can only occur from the PCV mode.

The property states that globally (G), if the PSV mode is selected (PSVModeSelected), then it must be true that PCV ventilation is currently active (PCVMode). This ensures that the system can only switch to PSV mode when PCV ventilation is active.

The property states that globally, if the system is in Pressure Controlled Ventilation (PCV) mode and actively ventilating, and the user initiates a switch to Pressure Support Ventilation (PSV) mode, then eventually the system will enter PSV mode. This directly captures the requirement that the system provides the capability to switch to PSV mode while PCV ventilation is active.

The property states that whenever the user initiates a switch to PSV mode (PSVModeSelected), it must be true that the system is currently in PCV mode (PCVMode). This captures the requirement that the means to switch from PCV to PSV are provided while PCV ventilation is active, meaning the switch can only occur when the system is operating in PCV mode.

The property states that globally (G), if the system is in Pressure Controlled Ventilation Mode (PCVMode) AND the user initiates a switch to PSV mode (PSVModeSelected), then eventually (F) the system will enter Pressure Support Ventilation Mode (PSVMode). This captures the requirement for the system to enable and execute the transition from PCV to PSV when the means (user selection) are provided while PCV is active.

The property states that globally, if the PCV (Pressure Controlled Ventilation) mode is active and the user initiates a switch to PSV (Pressure Support Ventilation) mode, then in the next state, the system must be in PSV mode. This captures the requirement that the system provides the means to transition from PCV to PSV while PCV is operational.

The property states that globally, if the system is in PCV mode and a switch to PSV mode is initiated, then eventually the system must enter PSV mode. This directly translates the requirement that means are provided to switch from PCV to PSV while PCV ventilation is active, implying that such an initiation leads to the PSV mode.

The property states that globally (G), if the system is currently in PCV mode (bool_exp1) and the user initiates a switch to PSV mode (bool_exp2), then in the very next state (X), the system will be in PSV mode (bool_exp3). This precisely captures the requirement for providing means to switch from PCV to PSV while PCV is active, implying an immediate transition upon selection.
